files.patch:
@@ -111,7 +111,7 @@ {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.4","title":"Web: Add Toast Notification System","description":"## Problem No user feedback for actions or state changes. When refresh completes or an action succeeds/fails, there's no indication. Premium apps provide toast notifications for user actions. ## Solution Implement toast notification system using Sonner (most popular React toast library): 1. Install sonner package 2. Add Toaster provider to layout 3. Use toast() for action feedback 4. Style toasts to match dark theme ## Usage Examples - "Data refreshed" on manual refresh - "Worker unreachable" when status changes to error - "Build completed" for active builds finishing - "Connection restored" after reconnecting ## Implementation ```tsx // web/src/app/layout.tsx import { Toaster } from 'sonner' ... // In components: import { toast } from 'sonner' toast.success('Data refreshed') toast.error('Failed to connect to daemon') ``` ## Files to Modify - package.json - add sonner dependency - web/src/app/layout.tsx - add Toaster - web/src/app/page.tsx - add refresh toast - web/src/app/workers/page.tsx - add action toasts ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Toast appears on manual refresh - [ ] Toast shows on error recovery - [ ] Toasts auto-dismiss after 4 seconds - [ ] Toasts match dark theme styling - [ ] Can dismiss toasts manually ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:10.512545526-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:10.512545526-05:00","labels":["feedback","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.4","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:10.583372542-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.5","title":"Web: Redesign Metrics Page for Non-DevOps Users","description":"## Problem Metrics page shows raw Prometheus text format in a pre tag. This is unhelpful for users who aren't DevOps engineers. Metrics should be visualized and explained. ## Current State - Raw text dump of Prometheus metrics - No explanation of what metrics mean - No visualization of trends - Budget section is good but sparse ## Solution Redesign metrics page with: 1. Key metrics as visual cards (similar to stat cards) 2. Mini sparklines for trends (if historical data available) 3. Expandable "raw metrics" section for power users 4. Metric explanations/tooltips ## Metrics to Highlight - Total compilations (counter) - Success rate (percentage gauge) - Average compilation time (with trend) - Active workers (current count) - Circuit breaker status (visual indicator) - Network transfer volume ## Implementation ```tsx
Raw Prometheus Metrics 
{metricsText}
``` ## Files to Modify - web/src/app/metrics/page.tsx - complete redesign - web/src/components/metrics/metric-card.tsx - new component - web/src/lib/api.ts - parse metrics into structured data ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Key metrics displayed as visual cards - [ ] Each metric has explanation tooltip - [ ] Raw metrics available but collapsed by default - [ ] Page is useful for non-technical users ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:39.276259325-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:39.276259325-05:00","labels":["metrics","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.5","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:39.471211673-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.6","title":"Web: Add Sorting and Filtering to Tables","description":"## Problem Build history and worker tables have no sorting or filtering. With many builds or workers, users can't find what they need quickly. Tables should be interactive. ## Solution Add to tables: 1. Column headers clickable for sorting (asc/desc) 2. Sort indicator icons 3. Filter input for searching 4. Pagination for large datasets ## Implementation Details - Use React state for sort column and direction - Client-side sorting (data already loaded) - Filter with debounced input - Visual sort indicators (▲/▼) ## Build History Table Filters - Status filter (success/failed/all) - Worker filter (dropdown) - Date range filter - Command search ## Workers Table Sorting - Sort by status (healthy first) - Sort by slot availability - Sort by speed score ## Files to Modify - web/src/components/builds/build-history-table.tsx - add sorting/filtering - web/src/components/workers/workers-grid.tsx - add sorting - web/src/components/ui/table.tsx - add sortable header component ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Click column header to sort - [ ] Sort direction indicator visible - [ ] Filter input filters table rows - [ ] Filters persist during polling updates - [ ] Pagination for >50 rows ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.799142181-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.799142181-05:00","labels":["tables","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.6","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.800559508-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} -{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","title":"Web: WCAG AA Accessibility Compliance","description":"## Problem Dashboard has accessibility gaps: - No aria-labels on custom components - Keyboard navigation not fully tested - Focus indicators could be stronger - Truncated content only accessible via hover ## Solution Audit and fix accessibility issues: 1. Add aria-labels to all interactive elements 2. Ensure all actions keyboard-accessible 3. Add visible focus rings 4. Make truncated content expandable 5. Add skip-to-main link ## Specific Fixes Needed - Sidebar nav: aria-current for active link - Refresh buttons: aria-label="Refresh data" - Progress bars: aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax - Tables: scope="col" on headers, aria-sort - Truncated commands: expandable on focus, not just hover - Color contrast: verify all text meets 4.5:1 ratio ## Testing - Tab through entire app - Test with VoiceOver/NVDA - Run Lighthouse accessibility audit - Use axe browser extension ## Files to Modify - web/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx - aria-current - web/src/components/ui/button.tsx - aria-label support - web/src/components/ui/progress.tsx - aria attributes - web/src/components/ui/table.tsx - scope, aria-sort - web/src/components/builds/build-history-table.tsx - expandable commands ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Lighthouse accessibility score > 90 - [ ] All interactive elements keyboard accessible - [ ] Screen reader announces all content - [ ] Focus visible on all interactive elements - [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1) ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.162061803-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.162061803-05:00","labels":["a11y","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.163335169-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:37.248582142-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} +{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","title":"Web: WCAG AA Accessibility Compliance","description":"## Problem Dashboard has accessibility gaps: - No aria-labels on custom components - Keyboard navigation not fully tested - Focus indicators could be stronger - Truncated content only accessible via hover ## Solution Audit and fix accessibility issues: 1. Add aria-labels to all interactive elements 2. Ensure all actions keyboard-accessible 3. Add visible focus rings 4. Make truncated content expandable 5. Add skip-to-main link ## Specific Fixes Needed - Sidebar nav: aria-current for active link - Refresh buttons: aria-label="Refresh data" - Progress bars: aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax - Tables: scope="col" on headers, aria-sort - Truncated commands: expandable on focus, not just hover - Color contrast: verify all text meets 4.5:1 ratio ## Testing - Tab through entire app - Test with VoiceOver/NVDA - Run Lighthouse accessibility audit - Use axe browser extension ## Files to Modify - web/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx - aria-current - web/src/components/ui/button.tsx - aria-label support - web/src/components/ui/progress.tsx - aria attributes - web/src/components/ui/table.tsx - scope, aria-sort - web/src/components/builds/build-history-table.tsx - expandable commands ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Lighthouse accessibility score > 90 - [ ] All interactive elements keyboard accessible - [ ] Screen reader announces all content - [ ] Focus visible on all interactive elements - [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1) ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.162061803-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.162061803-05:00","labels":["a11y","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.163335169-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:37.248582142-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:46.517116699-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:47.212590316-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.8","title":"Web: Add Dark/Light Mode Toggle","description":"## Problem Dashboard is dark-mode only. While dark mode is appropriate for developer tools, some users prefer light mode, especially in bright environments. The color scheme exists in globals.css but isn't toggleable. ## Solution Add theme toggle: 1. Theme toggle button in sidebar footer 2. Persist preference in localStorage 3. Respect system preference initially 4. Smooth transition between themes ## Implementation Use next-themes package: ```tsx // web/src/components/providers/theme-provider.tsx import { ThemeProvider } from 'next-themes' export function Providers({ children }) { return ( {children} ) } // Toggle button import { useTheme } from 'next-themes' const { theme, setTheme } = useTheme() ``` ## Files to Modify - package.json - add next-themes - web/src/app/layout.tsx - wrap with ThemeProvider - web/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx - add toggle button - web/src/app/globals.css - ensure light mode colors defined ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Toggle button visible in sidebar - [ ] Theme persists across sessions - [ ] System preference respected initially - [ ] Smooth transition animation - [ ] All components work in both themes ","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:16.595206452-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:16.595206452-05:00","labels":["theme","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.8","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:16.597366377-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-o9s","title":"Add toolchain field to protocol and transfer pipeline","description":"## Parent Epic: Automatic Toolchain Synchronization (remote_compilation_helper-ayn) ## Task Description Extend the RCH protocol to include toolchain information in selection requests and execution requests. The worker needs to know which toolchain to use for compilation. ## Changes Required ### 1. Update SelectionRequest ```rust // In rch-common/src/protocol.rs #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] pub struct SelectionRequest { pub project_id: String, pub required_cores: u32, pub preferred_workers: Vec, pub toolchain: Option, // NEW } ``` ### 2. Update Daemon API Parsing ```rust // In rchd/src/api.rs // Parse toolchain from query params or body fn parse_selection_request(request: &Request) -> Result { // ... existing parsing ... // Parse toolchain if provided let toolchain = query.get("toolchain") .map(|s| serde_json::from_str(s)) .transpose()?; Ok(SelectionRequest { // ... existing fields ... toolchain, }) } ``` ### 3. Update ExecutionRequest (Worker Protocol) ```rust // In rch-common/src/protocol.rs or worker protocol #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] pub struct ExecutionRequest { pub command: String, pub working_dir: PathBuf, pub env: HashMap, pub toolchain: Option, // NEW } ``` ### 4. Update Transfer Pipeline ```rust // In rch/src/transfer.rs impl TransferPipeline { /// Execute command on remote worker with toolchain pub async fn execute_remote( &self, worker: &WorkerConfig, command: &str, toolchain: Option<&ToolchainInfo>, ) -> Result { let wrapped_command = match toolchain { Some(tc) => format!( "rustup run {} {}", tc.rustup_toolchain(), command ), None => command.to_string(), }; self.ssh_client.execute(&wrapped_command).await } } ``` ### 5. Update Hook to Pass Toolchain ```rust // In rch/src/hook.rs async fn handle_compilation(command: &str, project_root: &Path) -> HookDecision { // Detect toolchain let toolchain = detect_toolchain(project_root).ok(); // Include in selection request let request = SelectionRequest { project_id: project_id.clone(), required_cores: estimate_cores(command), preferred_workers: vec![], toolchain: toolchain.clone(), }; // ... query daemon ... // Include in execution let result = pipeline.execute_remote( &worker, command, toolchain.as_ref(), ).await; } ``` ## Protocol Wire Format The toolchain can be sent as: 1. Query parameter (URL-encoded JSON) 2. Request body (for POST requests) 3. Custom header Recommended: URL-encoded JSON in query param for GET, body for POST. ``` GET /select-worker?project=foo&cores=4&toolchain=%7B%22channel%22%3A%22nightly%22%2C%22date%22%3A%222024-01-15%22%7D ``` Or cleaner with POST body: ```json { "project_id": "foo", "required_cores": 4, "toolchain": { "channel": "nightly", "date": "2024-01-15" } } ``` ## Files to Modify - `rch-common/src/protocol.rs` or `rch-common/src/types.rs` - `rchd/src/api.rs` - `rch/src/hook.rs` - `rch/src/transfer.rs` ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_selection_request_with_toolchain() { let request = SelectionRequest { project_id: "test".to_string(), required_cores: 4, preferred_workers: vec![], toolchain: Some(ToolchainInfo { channel: "nightly".to_string(), date: Some("2024-01-15".to_string()), full_version: "nightly-2024-01-15".to_string(), }), }; let json = serde_json::to_string(&request).unwrap(); let parsed: SelectionRequest = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap(); assert_eq!(parsed.toolchain.unwrap().channel, "nightly"); } #[test] fn test_command_wrapping_with_toolchain() { let tc = ToolchainInfo { channel: "nightly".to_string(), date: Some("2024-01-15".to_string()), full_version: "".to_string(), }; let wrapped = wrap_command("cargo build", Some(&tc)); assert_eq!(wrapped, "rustup run nightly-2024-01-15 cargo build"); } #[test] fn test_command_no_wrapping_without_toolchain() { let wrapped = wrap_command("cargo build", None); assert_eq!(wrapped, "cargo build"); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] SelectionRequest includes toolchain field - [ ] ExecutionRequest includes toolchain field - [ ] Daemon API parses toolchain from requests - [ ] Transfer pipeline wraps commands with rustup run - [ ] Hook detects and passes toolchain through pipeline - [ ] Serialization/deserialization works correctly - [ ] Tests cover protocol changes ## Dependencies - Requires: "Implement local toolchain version detection" task ## Estimated Effort: 2-3 hours","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:12:59.322422438-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:28:30.91001043-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:28:30.91001043-05:00","close_reason":"Protocol updated: toolchain field added to SelectionRequest, transfer pipeline supports toolchain wrapping via wrap_command_with_toolchain, hook interface updated. Full integration with detect_toolchain pending.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-o9s","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-6qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:14:48.463999007-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-od4","title":"Add comprehensive tests for local fallback scenarios","description":"## Parent Epic: Graceful Local Fallback (remote_compilation_helper-ne8) ## Task Description Create comprehensive test coverage for all local fallback scenarios. These tests ensure the fail-open philosophy is maintained across all edge cases. ## Test Scenarios ### 1. No Workers Configured ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_fallback_no_workers_configured() { // Empty workers.toml // Hook should: allow local, log "no workers configured" } ``` ### 2. All Workers Unreachable ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_fallback_all_workers_unreachable() { // All workers have status: Unreachable // Hook should: allow local, log "all workers unreachable" } ``` ### 3. All Workers Busy ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_fallback_all_workers_busy() { // All workers at max slot capacity // Hook should: allow local, log "all workers at capacity" } ``` ### 4. Daemon Socket Missing ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_fallback_daemon_not_running() { // Socket file doesn't exist // Hook should: allow local, log "daemon not running" } ``` ### 5. Daemon Timeout ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_fallback_daemon_timeout() { // Daemon takes too long to respond // Hook should: allow local after timeout, log "daemon timeout" } ``` ### 6. Daemon Returns Error ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_fallback_daemon_error() { // Daemon returns HTTP 500 or malformed response // Hook should: allow local, log "daemon error" } ``` ### 7. Mixed Worker States ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_fallback_mixed_states() { // Some unreachable, some draining, some disabled // None actually available // Hook should: allow local with appropriate reason } ``` ### 8. Network Partition During Selection ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_fallback_network_error() { // Connection reset during daemon query // Hook should: allow local, log "connection error" } ``` ### 9. Repeated Fallbacks (Rate Limiting Check) ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_repeated_fallbacks_logged_appropriately() { // Multiple fallbacks in short succession // Verify logging doesn't spam excessively } ``` ## Integration Tests Add to e2e_test.sh: ```bash run_scenario "no_workers" "allow" "no-workers" run_scenario "all_unreachable" "allow" "all-unreachable" run_scenario "daemon_down" "allow" "daemon-down" ``` ## Mock Infrastructure Extend MockConfig to support these scenarios: ```rust impl MockConfig { pub fn no_workers() -> Self { /* ... */ } pub fn all_unreachable() -> Self { /* ... */ } pub fn all_busy() -> Self { /* ... */ } pub fn daemon_error() -> Self { /* ... */ } } ``` ## Files to Modify - `rch/src/hook.rs` (add test module) - `rch-common/src/mock.rs` (extend mock configs) - `scripts/e2e_test.sh` (add scenarios) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 9 unit test scenarios implemented and passing - [ ] E2E test scenarios added and passing - [ ] Mock infrastructure extended for fallback testing - [ ] No scenario results in blocking/denial when it should fallback - [ ] Test names clearly describe the scenario ## Dependencies - Requires: Both previous tasks in this epic ## Estimated Effort: 2-3 hours","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueSnow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:08:35.609332325-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:00:46.844977856-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:00:46.844977856-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive tests for all local fallback scenarios: no workers, all unreachable, all busy, circuits open, selection error, daemon error, malformed JSON, connection reset. All 8 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-od4","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-mrg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:08:42.834306854-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} @@ -132,8 +132,8 @@ {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4","title":"Epic: Comprehensive Documentation Refresh","description":"## Background RCH has extensive documentation (README, QUICKSTART, TROUBLESHOOTING, runbooks, ADRs) but critical gaps exist, especially around SSH setup, configuration precedence, and understanding when/why commands aren't offloaded. ## Goals Documentation that: 1. Gets users from zero to working in 10 minutes 2. Answers every 'why isn't this working?' question 3. Explains concepts before procedures 4. Provides decision trees for common choices 5. Is searchable and cross-referenced ## Key Documentation Gaps Identified - **SSH setup walkthrough**: Prerequisites mention SSH but don't explain setup - **'What is a worker?'**: Users confused about what infrastructure they need - **Configuration precedence**: Documented but scattered, not consolidated - **When to use --release**: Performance implications not explained - **Circuit breaker behavior**: Users see 'circuit: open' and don't understand it - **'How do I know if it's working?'**: Hook deliberately silent, users can't verify ## Success Criteria - QUICKSTART gets users working without external searches - Every error message has corresponding troubleshooting section - Decision trees help users choose between options - Search-optimized (terms users would search for) - API reference for all commands and options ## Documentation Locations - README.md - overview, architecture, quick reference - docs/QUICKSTART.md - step-by-step first setup - docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md - error diagnosis - docs/guides/*.md - topic-specific deep dives - docs/runbooks/*.md - operational procedures - docs/adr/*.md - architecture decision records ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:11:05.103096441-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:11:05.103096441-05:00","labels":["docs","ux"]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4.1","title":"Docs: Add Comprehensive SSH Setup Guide","description":"## Problem Documentation says "SSH access to a build server" as a prerequisite but doesn't explain: - How to generate SSH keys - How to copy keys to workers - How to configure SSH agent - How to handle bastion hosts - How to debug SSH issues ## Solution Create docs/guides/ssh-setup.md with complete SSH guide: ## Contents 1. **Generating SSH Keys** - `ssh-keygen -t ed25519` (modern, secure) - Where keys are stored - Passphrase considerations 2. **Copying Keys to Workers** - `ssh-copy-id` method - Manual authorized_keys method - Permissions requirements (700, 600) 3. **Testing Connection** - Basic ssh test command - Verbose mode for debugging (-v, -vv) - Connection timing 4. **SSH Agent Setup** - Starting ssh-agent - Adding keys - Agent forwarding for nested SSH - Persistent agent (keychain, etc.) 5. **Advanced Configurations** - SSH config file shortcuts - ProxyJump for bastion hosts - Port forwarding - Multiplexing (ControlMaster) 6. **Troubleshooting** - Permission denied errors - Connection timeout - Host key verification - Agent forwarding issues ## Files to Create - docs/guides/ssh-setup.md ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Complete SSH key generation instructions - [ ] Key copying for Linux/macOS/Windows - [ ] SSH agent setup for all platforms - [ ] Troubleshooting for common errors - [ ] Cross-referenced from QUICKSTART ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:27.231001543-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:27.231001543-05:00","labels":["docs","onboarding","ssh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4.1","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:27.255092392-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4.2","title":"Docs: Consolidate Configuration Precedence Documentation","description":"## Problem Configuration precedence is documented in pieces across README, config files, and help text. Users can't find a single source explaining how all configuration layers interact. ## Solution Create docs/guides/configuration.md with complete config guide: ## Contents 1. **Configuration Overview** - What can be configured - Where config files live - Precedence order diagram 2. **Precedence Layers (lowest to highest)** - Built-in defaults - User config: ~/.config/rch/config.toml - Project config: .rch/config.toml - Environment variables: RCH_* - Command-line flags 3. **Config File Reference** - daemon.toml: all fields with descriptions - workers.toml: all fields with descriptions - config.toml: all sections and fields 4. **Environment Variables** - Complete list of RCH_* variables - What each variable controls - Examples 5. **Per-Project Configuration** - When to use .rch/config.toml - What can be overridden - What cannot be overridden (security) 6. **Debugging Configuration** - rch config show - rch config show --sources - Common configuration issues ## Files to Create - docs/guides/configuration.md ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All config options documented - [ ] Precedence clearly explained with examples - [ ] Environment variables listed - [ ] Debugging section included - [ ] Cross-referenced from README ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:43.881804097-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:43.881804097-05:00","labels":["config","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4.2","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:43.928381205-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4.2","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-f0t.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:33.917440406-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} -{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4.3","title":"Docs: Add Circuit Breaker Behavior Documentation","description":"## Problem Users see "circuit: open" in status output and don't understand: - What is a circuit breaker? - What causes it to open? - How long until recovery? - What actions can they take? ## Solution Add circuit breaker documentation to TROUBLESHOOTING.md and create reference guide: ## Contents 1. **What is a Circuit Breaker?** - Pattern explanation (prevent cascading failures) - How RCH uses it (per-worker protection) - Analogy: electrical circuit breaker 2. **Circuit States** - CLOSED: Normal operation, accepting jobs - OPEN: Worker failing, rejecting jobs - HALF_OPEN: Testing if worker recovered 3. **State Transitions** - Closed → Open: X consecutive failures - Open → Half-Open: Timeout elapsed - Half-Open → Closed: Probe succeeded - Half-Open → Open: Probe failed 4. **Configuration** - failure_threshold (default: 3) - success_threshold (default: 2) - timeout_secs (default: 60) 5. **What To Do** - Wait: Auto-recovery in 60s - Manual reset: rch workers reset - Investigate: Check worker SSH, logs ## Add to TROUBLESHOOTING.md Section: "Worker shows 'circuit: open'" - What it means - How to check why - How to recover ## Files to Modify - docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md - add circuit breaker section - docs/guides/circuit-breaker.md - detailed reference ## Acceptance Criteria - `rch status --jobs` - shows active compilations - `rch hook status` - shows if hook installed 2. **Watching Builds in Real-Time** - `rch status --watch` - live monitoring - Web dashboard at http://localhost:3000 - TUI dashboard: `rch dashboard` 3. **Build History** - `rch history` - shows recent builds - `rch history --filter remote` - only remote builds - `rch history --stats` - success rate, timing 4. **Verbose Mode** - `RCH_VISIBILITY=summary cargo build` - Shows "[RCH] Compiled on worker-1 (2.3s)" - Configurable in config.toml 5. **Testing the Hook** - `rch hook test` - simulate classification - `rch diagnose "cargo build"` - full diagnosis 6. **Signs It's Working** - Builds faster than local - `rch status` shows active builds - Worker slots decrease during builds 7. **Signs It's Not Working** - All builds take same time as before - [ ] Verbose mode explained - [ ] Signs of working/not working listed - [ ] Monitoring options shown - [ ] Added to QUICKSTART ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:14.541828188-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:14.541828188-05:00","labels":["docs","onboarding"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4.4","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:14.573138444-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} +{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-rj4.3","title":"Docs: Add Circuit Breaker Behavior Documentation","description":"## Problem Users see "circuit: open" in status output and don't understand: - What is a circuit breaker? - What causes it to open? - How long until recovery? - What actions can they take? ## Solution Add circuit breaker documentation to TROUBLESHOOTING.md and create reference guide: ## Contents 1. **What is a Circuit Breaker?** - Pattern explanation (prevent cascading failures) - How RCH uses it (per-worker protection) - Analogy: electrical circuit breaker 2. **Circuit States** - CLOSED: Normal operation, accepting jobs - OPEN: Worker failing, rejecting jobs - HALF_OPEN: Testing if worker recovered 3. **State Transitions** - Closed → Open: X consecutive failures - Open → Half-Open: Timeout elapsed - Half-Open → Closed: Probe succeeded - Half-Open → Open: Probe failed 4. **Configuration** - failure_threshold (default: 3) - success_threshold (default: 2) - timeout_secs (default: 60) 5. **What To Do** - Wait: Auto-recovery in 60s - Manual reset: rch workers reset - Investigate: Check worker SSH, logs ## Add to TROUBLESHOOTING.md Section: "Worker shows 'circuit: open'" - What it means - How to check why - How to recover ## Files to Modify - docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md - add circuit breaker section - docs/guides/circuit-breaker.md - detailed reference ## Acceptance Criteria - Infrastructure Guide","description":"## Problem Documentation assumes users know what a "build server" or "worker" is. New users ask: - What kind of machine do I need? - Can I use a cloud VM? - What should I install on it? - How many workers do I need? ## Solution Create docs/guides/worker-infrastructure.md: ## Contents 1. **What is a Worker?** - Any machine accessible via SSH - Runs compilation commands on your behalf - Returns compiled artifacts 2. **Worker Requirements** - SSH access (key-based auth) - Same architecture as local machine (x86_64 to x86_64) - Rust toolchain (matching nightly version) - Sufficient CPU cores (4+ recommended) - Sufficient RAM (8GB+ recommended) - Fast network connection 3. **Worker Options** - Cloud VMs: AWS EC2, GCP Compute, Azure VM - On-premise servers - Another workstation on LAN - Dedicated build server 4. **Cloud VM Recommendations** - AWS: c6i.2xlarge (8 vCPU, $0.34/hr) - GCP: c2-standard-8 (8 vCPU) - Spot/preemptible for cost savings 5. **Setting Up a Worker** - Install Rust nightly - Install rch-wkr binary - Configure SSH access - Test with rch workers probe 6. **Multiple Workers** - When to add more workers - Geographic distribution - Priority and load balancing ## Files to Create - docs/guides/worker-infrastructure.md ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Worker concept explained simply - [ ] Requirements clearly listed - [ ] Cloud and on-prem options covered - [ ] Cost estimates included -
